rulings.Rulings: hearing. apperance required to address whether interest is simple or compound. no objections have been filed. the issue re whether the note is due does not appear to be properly before the court as the petition states the petitioner does not dispute that payment is now due, and only asks for a determination of how interest should be calculated. the supplement filed 7/18/18 (no POS) raises the issue of whether payment is due for the first time.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HELEN FESKO EXEMPT TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Of Lack Of Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: The motion to dismiss is denied. The Court clearly has subject matter jurisdiction over the subject petition (and David Fesko's responding petition). The Court also has personal jurisdiction over Ms. Merriman given that the alleged claims arise at least in part from her conduct in California. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, July 24, 2018.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JACK FESKO EXEMPT TRUST

Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, July 24, 2018.
